page: OVHcloud a10-180 is a GPU-accelerated server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9554 processor, delivering 120 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz with one thread per core. The system is equipped with 170.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.42 GB per core. For acceleration, it bundles four NVIDIA A10 GPUs from the Ampere family, yielding a total of 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as PassMark CPU and memory scores. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation via Clang also rank in the top tier. For database workloads, the system demonstrates top-tier efficiency with Redis SET operations reaching 151.11 million operations per second. Large language model (LLM) inference benchmarks show top-tier speeds for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

Given its high core count and quad-GPU configuration, the server offers high resource density for parallel processing tasks. However, the memory-to-core ratio is relatively low at 1.42 GB per core, which represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive workloads that do not leverage GPU acceleration. Qualitatively, the hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads that can fully utilize the combined power of 120 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 96 GB of NVIDIA VRAM. It is highly suited for LLM inference, machine learning training, parallel graphics rendering, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations.
description: The OVHcloud a10-180 is a high-density GPU-accelerated server designed for parallel computing and machine learning workloads. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9554 processor, it features 120 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz, paired with 170.0 GB of system memory. The server integrates four NVIDIA A10 Ampere GPUs, providing a total of 96 GB of VRAM, alongside a 400 GB local SSD and a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, Redis database operations, and LLM inference processing. While the memory-to-core ratio of 1.42 GB is relatively low, the server offers excellent resource density for compute-heavy tasks. It is highly suited for large language model inference, parallel software compilation, and GPU-accelerated data processing.
og_description: OVHcloud a10-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 120 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 170 GB RAM, four NVIDIA A10 GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM, and a 400 GB SSD for high-performance parallel computing.
meta_description: OVHcloud a10-180 is a GPU-accelerated server with 120 AMD EPYC cores, 170 GB RAM, four NVIDIA A10 GPUs, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density compute platform combining 120 dedicated AMD EPYC cores with quad NVIDIA A10 GPUs for accelerated parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 120 dedicated AMD EPYC 9554 physical cores running at 3.3 GHz
Four NVIDIA A10 Ampere GPUs providing 96 GB of total VRAM
170.0 GB system memory and 400 GB local SSD storage
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and multi-core CPU benchmarks
Best suited for machine learning, software compilation, and parallel processing
